input_dict.agent_role: Summarize first-hop evidence and expose the bridge entity, missing fact, or retrieval direction needed for the next hop.
input_dict.agent_function: Pipeline position: after first-hop retrieval and before second-hop query generation. Inputs are the original question and first-hop passages. Functional responsibility: compress grounded first-hop evidence into an intermediate state that helps the next agent identify and retrieve the missing second-hop information. This agent is not responsible for producing the final answer, and its state may normally describe an unresolved bridge.
input_dict.native_output_contract: The prompt must instruct the module to consume question and passages and produce one summary field.
input_dict.base_prompt: Given the fields `question`, `passages`, produce the fields `summary`.
input_dict.integrated_feedback:
{
  "hypothesis_decision": "retain",
  "semantic_boundary": "Change behavior when the question's requested relation or final answer requires composing or comparing facts that may be distributed across pages (e.g., birth-date comparisons, publication/founding years, ordinal counting that includes prequels, or verifying a candidate director among named options) AND the provided first-hop passages do not unambiguously supply every fact needed to resolve that relation for all relevant arguments. Do not change behavior when the passages already contain all required facts unambiguously and directly associated with the intended entities (single-hop cases or fully-resolved multi-argument facts). This boundary is defined by semantic completeness/ambiguity of the relation arguments, not by surface tokens, punctuation, particular strings, or the mere presence of numeric tokens or keywords.",
  "counterexample_analysis": "The matched positive evidence are all cases where the summarizer prematurely emitted an asserted final answer instead of exposing a bridge entity, the missing fact(s), and explicit next-hop retrieval guidance. Each counterexample enforces the hypothesis's semantic condition: in these examples the relation required evidence from multiple pages or disambiguation (missing candidate pages, multiple candidate persons named, or a needed authoritative page), so the summarizer must defer and emit a structured first-hop report. None of the counterexamples show a case where the passages were decisively complete and a final answer was appropriate; instead they highlight that summaries must be neutral, minimal, and directive when any argument of the relation is incomplete or ambiguous. They therefore support the provisional mechanism and emphasize stricter output constraints (minimal, non-assertive phrasing and explicit retrieval directives) rather than a change to the hypothesis's semantic scope.",
  "integrated_feedback": "Keep the core hypothesis: when a relation depends on facts spread across pages or when any relation argument is ambiguous/missing, summary1 must expose per-argument evidence, flag ambiguities/missing facts, and give prioritized concrete next-hop retrieval targets — and must not produce the final answer. Clarify and tighten the output contract: require labeled, minimal elements (per-entity evidence, ambiguity/missing-fact lines, prioritized next-hop targets/queries) and forbid assertive final-answer phrasing in these incomplete/ambiguous cases. Permit concise derived answers only when all required facts for the relation are present unambiguously in the supplied passages. Favor a compact, actionable format (short labeled fields or a one-paragraph/bullet list) rather than long prose.",
  "update_directions": [
    "When the relation in the question requires combining facts across pages OR any argument of the relation is ambiguous/missing in the provided passages, require summary1 to emit a short structured report containing: (a) evidence found per named target (which passage/title mentioned which candidate and any explicit fact present); (b) explicit ambiguity flags naming multiple candidate pages or missing expected pages; (c) the specific missing fact(s)/disambiguation needed to complete the relation; and (d) 1–4 prioritized next-hop retrieval targets or concise queries. In such cases the summary must use neutral, non-assertive phrasing (no final comparison, numeric answer, or definitive selection). Only allow a compact final answer from summary1 when the passages unambiguously contain all facts required to resolve the relation for every argument."
  ],
  "preservation_constraints": [
    "Do not prevent concise single-hop answers when the provided passages unambiguously contain the full facts needed to answer the question.",
    "Preserve the agent's ability to extract and quote short factual snippets and to identify passage titles/tokens as evidence.",
    "Avoid forcing extraneous verbosity or unnecessary retrievals in cases that are already complete and unambiguous.",
    "Retain the agent's existing single-field output contract (produce the `summary` field only)."
  ],
  "edit_recommendations": [
    {
      "action": "clarify",
      "target_behavior": "Detecting when to defer vs. answer",
      "proposed_change": "Add an explicit detection rule: if any relation argument (person, work, date, ordinal position, numeric comparator) is not unambiguously resolved by the provided passages (e.g., multiple candidate pages returned, a candidate page missing, or only one side of a comparison present), then treat the hop as incomplete and follow the structured deferral report; otherwise allow a concise derived result.",
      "reason": "This makes the semantic boundary explicit so the agent will not over-defer in clear single-hop cases and will defer precisely when the relation truly depends on cross-page composition or disambiguation."
    },
    {
      "action": "clarify",
      "target_behavior": "Structured summary contents and minimal phrasing",
      "proposed_change": "Specify the required labeled elements for incomplete/ambiguous cases: evidence: per-entity listing (passage title + short quoted token/fact), ambiguities/missing: named candidates or missing pages, missing_facts/bridge: exact fact(s) needed, retrieval_direction: 1–4 prioritized page titles or concise queries. Require neutral wording (e.g., 'Bridge entity: X; Retrieval: confirm Y') and forbid assertive language like 'X did Y' or single-token final answers.",
      "reason": "Counterexamples demand stricter, minimal and directive formatting so downstream hops get actionable signals and the summarizer cannot leak a premature final relation."
    },
    {
      "action": "keep",
      "target_behavior": "Concise factual extraction from passages",
      "proposed_change": "",
      "reason": "Existing extraction of short quoted fragments and citation of passage titles is useful and should be preserved; the recommended edits augment but do not replace this capability."
    }
  ],
  "rejected_local_patterns": [
    "Triggering deferral solely on the presence of digit tokens, parenthetical years, or keywords like 'born' without semantic ambiguity checks.",
    "Relying on surface title tokens (e.g., 'disambiguation', common surnames) or enumerated lists of names as the sole signal to defer.",
    "Forcing a single fixed header string, exact template phrases, or positional token rules as the only accepted output format.",
    "Substituting semantic reasoning with punctuation, wrapper removal, or token-position heuristics to decide to defer."
  ],
  "new_capability_required": false,
  "length_increase_justified": false,
  "generalization_rationale": "The revision is based on a semantic principle—require exposing missing relation arguments and retrieval intent whenever a question's relation cannot be resolved from the visible passages. That principle applies across relation types (comparisons, dates, ordinals, authorship, credits, founding years) and will generalize to other multi-hop scenarios where the second hop must resolve an ambiguous or missing fact.",
  "generalization_check": "This direction is semantic (completeness/ambiguity of relation arguments) rather than sample-indexed; it does not specify sample IDs, surface tokens, or fixed answer strings and therefore should transfer to other unseen examples that share the same semantic characteristics."
}

request.rendered_prompt:
You are rewriting one agent prompt in a fixed multi-agent HotpotQA
pipeline.

You receive only:
1. the current base prompt;
2. one already-synthesized integrated feedback object.

You do not have access to the raw optimization examples. Do not reconstruct
or invent sample-specific examples.

Produce one complete, directly usable shared prompt.

Requirements:
- Preserve the native input/output interface exactly.
- Start from the supplied base prompt and make the smallest coherent update
  justified by the integrated feedback.
- Keep useful base behavior that is not contradicted or superseded.
- Prefer revising, clarifying, replacing, or merging existing instructions
  when they already address the same behavior.
- Add a new instruction when the feedback identifies a genuinely missing
  capability that cannot be expressed clearly by modifying existing text.
- Avoid adding a separate rule block when the change can be integrated into
  the current structure.
- Prompt length may increase when a genuinely new capability is required.
  Any increase should be proportional to the new information, while
  redundant, overlapping, or superseded instructions should be removed or
  merged where practical.
- Preserve multiple rules when they represent genuinely independent
  behaviors, but avoid separate rules for individual examples, answer types,
  lexical patterns, or incidental formatting properties.
- Treat edit recommendations as an edit plan, not as text to append
  verbatim.
- When revision metadata is present, honor the semantic boundary and the
  retain/narrow/replace decision. Do not re-expand rejected local patterns
  into lexical rules or exception lists.
- Do not copy optimization terminology into the final prompt.
- Do not mention feedback, training, attribution, batches, examples,
  endpoints, deltas, metrics, or prompt optimization.
- Do not add proper nouns, document titles, answers, locations, literal
  case labels, or sample-specific exception lists.
- Keep the final prompt complete and self-contained.
- Do not refer to an omitted original prompt or unwritten rules.

Condition:
endpoint_delta_custom_signed

Agent:
summary1

Agent role:
Summarize first-hop evidence and expose the bridge entity, missing fact, or retrieval direction needed for the next hop.

Agent function and pipeline position:
Pipeline position: after first-hop retrieval and before second-hop query generation. Inputs are the original question and first-hop passages. Functional responsibility: compress grounded first-hop evidence into an intermediate state that helps the next agent identify and retrieve the missing second-hop information. This agent is not responsible for producing the final answer, and its state may normally describe an unresolved bridge.

Native output contract:
The prompt must instruct the module to consume question and passages and produce one summary field.

Base prompt:
Given the fields `question`, `passages`, produce the fields `summary`.

Return strict JSON only:
{
  "updated_prompt": "complete directly usable shared prompt",
  "clarified_instructions": [
    "existing behavior clarified or narrowed"
  ],
  "merged_instructions": [
    "overlapping behaviors consolidated"
  ],
  "replaced_instructions": [
    "incorrect or overly broad behavior replaced"
  ],
  "added_instructions": [
    "genuinely missing capability added"
  ],
  "remaining_conflicts": [
    "unresolved conflict, or an empty list"
  ],
  "rationale": "brief explanation of the edit strategy"
}

response:
{
  "updated_prompt": "Given the fields `question` and `passages`, produce the single field `summary`.

Goal: compress the grounded first-hop evidence into a short intermediate state that makes it easy for the next-hop agent to (a) see what facts are present per candidate/entity, (b) see what is ambiguous or missing, and (c) know 1–4 prioritized next-hop retrieval targets or concise queries. This module does not produce the final answer unless the provided passages already unambiguously contain every fact required to resolve the question's relation for every argument.

Procedure:
1) Decide whether the question's relation is semantically complete given the supplied passages. Treat the hop as INCOMPLETE if any of the following hold: a) the relation requires combining or comparing facts across pages (e.g., comparing birth dates, publication/founding years, ordinal ranks, confirming a named director among candidates) and the passages do not supply each required fact unambiguously for all relevant arguments; b) any relation argument is ambiguous or has multiple candidate pages or mentions in the passages; c) an expected candidate page or explicit fact for an argument is missing (e.g., only one side of a comparison is present). Do not trigger INCOMPLETE solely from surface tokens (years, digits) — base the decision on whether the relation's arguments are unambiguously resolved.

2) If the hop is COMPLETE (all required facts to resolve the relation for every argument are present unambiguously in the passages): produce a concise derived result in `summary`. This may be a short phrase or sentence that states the necessary fact(s) needed by the pipeline. Keep it minimal and grounded in the passages; you may include a 1–2 token citation (passage title) if helpful.

3) If the hop is INCOMPLETE or AMBIGUOUS: do NOT assert a final answer or perform the final comparison/selection. Instead emit a short structured report in `summary` containing these labeled elements (prefer bullets or a compact paragraph with explicit labels). Keep all text neutral and non-assertive.

  - evidence: For each named candidate/argument mentioned in the question or passages, list the candidate label and the exact passage title(s) that mention it plus a very short quoted snippet or explicit fact token found there (1–12 words). If a candidate is not mentioned, say so briefly.

  - ambiguities_or_missing: Explicitly name any ambiguities (e.g., multiple candidates for the same name, multiple pages that could be the intended entity) and any missing expected pages or facts.

  - missing_facts_or_bridge: State exactly which fact(s) or disambiguation is needed to complete the relation (e.g., "birth date for Person A", "publication year of Work B", "director credit for Film C"). Be as specific and minimal as possible.

  - retrieval_direction: Provide 1–4 prioritized next-hop targets or concise queries to resolve the missing facts/ambiguities. Prefer explicit page titles when available (exact if known), otherwise provide short search-style queries (few words) or a clear description of the document to retrieve. Order targets by priority.

Formatting and style constraints (apply to both COMPLETE and INCOMPLETE cases):
- The output must be a single `summary` field only. Do not emit other fields.
- In INCOMPLETE reports, use neutral, non-assertive phrasing. Do not produce final comparisons, numeric answers, or definitive selections (e.g., avoid "X is the oldest" or "Answer: 3").
- Keep the report compact and actionable (short labeled bullets or one concise paragraph). Avoid long prose.
- It is allowed and encouraged to quote short factual snippets from passages and cite passage titles to show grounding.
- Do not defer or mark INCOMPLETE when the passages already unambiguously provide all facts for the relation and its arguments.

Produce the `summary` content that follows these rules.",
  "clarified_instructions": [
    "Explicit detection rule for when to defer vs. produce a concise derived result: defer if any relation argument is ambiguous/missing or the relation requires cross-page composition and not all facts are unambiguously present.",
    "When deferring, require labeled elements: per-entity evidence, ambiguities/missing, exact missing facts/bridge, and 1–4 prioritized retrieval targets/queries.",
    "Require neutral, non-assertive phrasing in incomplete/ambiguous reports and forbid final-answer assertions in those cases."
  ],
  "merged_instructions": [
    "Preserved and merged the agent's ability to quote short factual snippets and cite passage titles into the structured-report requirements rather than as a separate capability.",
    "Consolidated instructions about compactness, grounding, and single-field output into unified formatting and style constraints."
  ],
  "replaced_instructions": [
    "Replaced the base prompt's implicit unconstrained summarization guidance with an explicit semantic completeness check and a two-branch output rule (COMPLETE vs INCOMPLETE) to prevent premature final answers when cross-page composition or ambiguity is required."
  ],
  "added_instructions": [
    "A clear detection rule listing semantic conditions that make a hop INCOMPLETE (cross-page composition needed, ambiguous/multiple candidates, missing expected pages or facts) and forbidding triggers based only on surface tokens.",
    "Detailed required structure for INCOMPLETE summaries: evidence, ambiguities_or_missing, missing_facts_or_bridge, retrieval_direction with examples of what each must contain."
  ],
  "remaining_conflicts": [],
  "rationale": "Added a narrow semantic completeness test and a compact structured deferral report so the summarizer exposes per-argument evidence, exact missing facts, and prioritized next-hop queries when relations require cross-page composition or disambiguation; preserved concise single-hop behavior and the ability to quote passages while preventing premature final answers."
}
